urgency.message: 89 critical CVE(s) and 5 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: When Information Becomes the Attack Surface – Understanding AI Agent Traps
items.summary:
From hidden content injections to cognitive state poisoning, attackers are turning trusted data sources into traps for autonomous AI.
The post When Information Becomes the Attack Surface – Understanding AI Agent Traps appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA Warns Critical Lantronix EDS5000 Flaw Is Being Actively Exploited
items.summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday warned of active exploitation of a critical security flaw impacting Lantronix EDS5000 Series devices, urging Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to apply the fixes by June 26, 2026.

The vulnerability in questi

items.title: More Malicious OpenClaw Skills Threaten AI Supply Chain
items.summary: OpenClaw removed five packages from ClawHub, its skills marketplace, that bypassed security checks even though they included infostealers and other threats.

items.title: Researchers Trick AI Browsers Into Leaking Credentials
items.summary: LayerX tricked AI browsers including ChatGPT Atlas and Comet into bypassing their guardrails

items.title: Amadey and StealC Malware Network Disrupted, 27M Stolen Credentials Recovered
items.summary:
A coordinated law enforcement operation, in partnership with private sector companies, including Bitdefender, Bitsight, ESET, and Microsoft, has resulted in the takedown of criminal infrastructure powering Amadey and StealC.

"The main common goal was to disrupt the 'assembly lines' cybercriminals u

items.title: Europol-Led Operation Endgame Takes Down StealC and Amadey Infostealers
items.summary: Operation Endgame seized around 50 domains and nearly 200 active IP-based servers associated with the infostealers

items.title: Microsoft and Allies Smash Shared Infrastructure of Amadey and StealC Malware
items.summary:
Hundreds of C&C servers were disrupted in an operation involving law enforcement and several cybersecurity companies.
The post Microsoft and Allies Smash Shared Infrastructure of Amadey and StealC Malware appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA warns of max severity Ubiquiti flaws exploited in attacks
items.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is warning of hackers actively exploiting flaws in Ubiquity UniFi OS and Lantronix serial-to-ethernet servers. [...]

items.title: Amadey, StealC malware operations disrupted in Operation Endgame action
items.summary: Microsoft, Europol, and international partners have disrupted infrastructure used by the Amadey and StealC malware operations as part of Operation Endgame, which targets cybercriminal services and ransomware gangs. [...]

items.title: Exclusive: Meet AIVEX, a New Triage Model Built to Reduce Supply Chain Threat and Risk
items.summary:
The new framework seeks to help security teams identify which software supply chain vulnerabilities pose the greatest operational, safety, and business risks in AI-driven environments.

items.title: German rail services resume after wireless communications outage
items.summary: Deutsche Bahn said a nationwide disruption of railway services was tied to a malfunction in its 2G-based GSM-R communications system.

items.title: Securing the service desk: Why social engineering attacks keep succeeding
items.summary: Service desks have become a favored target for attackers seeking password resets, MFA changes, and access to corporate accounts. Specops Software breaks down how service desk social engineering attacks work and how organizations can defend against them. [...]

items.title: macOS Backdoor Uses Prompt Injection to Evade AI Triage
items.summary: SentinelLabs found a North Korea-linked macOS backdoor using prompt injection on AI triage tools

items.title: macOS Weaknesses Chained to Silently Disable Endpoint Security Agents
items.summary:
A standard non-admin account is sufficient to conduct an attack that exploits legitimate OS behavior rather than software vulnerabilities.
The post macOS Weaknesses Chained to Silently Disable Endpoint Security Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Indian auto giant Bajaj Auto hit by ransomware incident
items.summary: The company said in a regulatory filing that it became aware of the incident on Tuesday morning and had taken precautionary measures to contain its impact.

items.title: How much cyber risk does AI create for organizations? 457 million security issues. Here’s what you can do about it.
items.summary: Over a 30 day period, Tenable detected 457 million AI-related security issues among 7,000-plus organizations, an average of 62,000 exposures per organization. If we didn’t already know that shadow AI was a problem, data like this makes it clear every organization needs to visualize, map, assess, and

items.title: Third DraftKings Hacker Sentenced to 18 Months in Prison
items.summary:
Nathan Austad has been ordered to pay roughly $1.8 million in forfeiture and restitution, and the sentence also includes 3 years of supervised release. 
The post Third DraftKings Hacker Sentenced to 18 Months in Prison appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Expose 300+ GitHub Repositories to Supply-Chain Attacks
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new class of CI/CD workflow weakness that allows attackers to hijack workflows and compromise open-source supply chains.

The "critical exploitable pattern" has been codenamed Cordyceps by Novee Security. The issue can allow full attacker control of repositor

items.title: KDDI Breach Affects Six Japanese ISPs, Exposes 14.2 Email Credentials
items.summary: Customers of the affected Japanese email services are “strongly advised” to change their email passwords

items.title: Critical Ubiquiti Vulnerabilities in Attackers’ Crosshairs
items.summary:
The flaws allow remote, unauthenticated attackers to make system changes, access underlying accounts, and inject commands.
The post Critical Ubiquiti Vulnerabilities in Attackers’ Crosshairs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Agentic AI Security: Wrong Context, Wrong Decisions at Machine Speed
items.summary:
Context is the central plank of AI in general, and agentic AI in particular. If an AI system doesn’t have the correct context, it cannot make the correct decisions.
The post Agentic AI Security: Wrong Context, Wrong Decisions at Machine Speed appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Apple's MacOS Gap Lets Users Disable Security Tools
items.summary: Attackers can exploit the issue to disable security and integrated browser tools without needing administrator privileges or kernel exploits.

items.title: Iran-Linked MuddyWater Poses as Ransomware Gang to Mask Cyber Espionage
items.summary: An NCC Group report warns state-backed hackers are attempting to hide activity by posing as ransomware groups and deploying commercially available malware

items.title: New ‘Mistic’ RAT Opens Door to Several Ransomware Families
items.summary:
Mistic is used by Woodgnat, an initial access broker working with Qilin, Interlock, Rhysida, Akira, 8Base, and Black Basta.
The post New ‘Mistic’ RAT Opens Door to Several Ransomware Families appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Dawn of the Apex Agentic Adversary
items.summary:
We are standing at the end of an era we never thought to mourn: the era of human-speed threats.

For years, cybersecurity moved to a rhythm organizations could follow. A researcher found a bug, a CVE was cataloged, a vendor navigated a patch cycle, and weeks or even months later, a fix was deployed.

items.title: Tata Electronics confirms cyberattack as hackers leak data
items.summary: Tata Electronics has confirmed in a statement to BleepingComputer that it was the target of a cyberattack that impacted parts of its IT infrastructure. [...]

items.title: Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data
items.summary: More victims have emerged after attackers breached application vendor Klue and used its OAuth tokens to steal customers' Salesforce data.

items.title: Windows 11 KB5095093 update rolls out new Point-in-Time restore feature
items.summary: ​​Microsoft has released the KB5095093 preview cumulative update for Windows 11 24H2 and 25H2, which fixes numerous bugs and begins rolling out new features, including the new Point-in-Time restore feature. [...]

items.title: Healthtech firm Xolis suffers data breach impacting 1.4 million people
items.summary: Healthcare technology company Xsolis says that sensitive data belonging to nearly 1.4 million individuals was compromised in a phishing attack that gave attackers access to its network. [...]

items.title: Dialog Claims It Was Hacked. A Misconfigured Website Left Its Members Exposed
items.summary: The private events group, cofounded by Peter Thiel, says a “criminal” hacker is behind a breach that exposed members’ personal details. WIRED found no evidence a break-in was needed to access the files.

items.title: Five Eyes agencies sound alarm about AI’s threat to cybersecurity
items.summary: "The timeline is not years, it is months,” the nations of the Five Eyes intelligence alliance said in a joint alert about the cybersecurity concerns of artificial intelligence.

items.title: FortiBleed Targeted FortiGate Firewalls in 110 Million-Credential Harvesting Operation
items.summary:
A Russian-speaking initial access broker (IAB) driven by financial gain is assessed to be behind a large-scale credential-harvesting operation known as FortiBleed that has targeted over 430,000 FortiGate firewalls globally.

items.title: Feds seize alleged cyber-scam infrastructure connected to Southeast Asian company
items.summary: The Department of Justice announced the “seizure of a cloud computing account” used by subsidiaries of the Huione Group, a conglomerate severed from the U.S. financial system last year.

items.title: Trump directs federal agencies to protect US data from quantum threats
items.summary: An executive order signed Monday aims to accelerate the government's transition to post-quantum cryptography (PQC), a new generation of encryption designed to protect data from the powerful quantum computers expected in the future.

items.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
items.summary: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. items.title: Fake AI Agent Skill Passed Security Scans and Reportedly Reached 26,000 Agents
items.summary:
Security firm AIR built a fake AI agent skill, pushed it through a popular skill marketplace and an Instagram ad, and says it reached roughly 26,000 agents, including some on corporate accounts.
